Therefore, it may be reclaimed if not part of the designated curriculum, ensuring the prudent use of resources and preventing national loss."
<|det|>text [241, 847, 428, 894]<|/det|>Published and Distributed by Orange House Pvt Ltd (Benchmark Press Pvt Ltd)
<PAGE><|det|>title [99, 136, 331, 183]<|/det|>Preface
<|det|>image [532, 159, 640, 201]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [405, 200, 519, 245]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [140, 288, 666, 312]<|/det|>"Our values inform our thoughts, words and actions."
<|det|>text [139, 314, 859, 371]<|/det|>Values reflect our sense of right and wrong. They help us grow and develop. They help us create the future we want. The decisions we make everyday are a reflection of our values.
<|det|>text [140, 376, 860, 470]<|/det|>In today's world, it may seem our society doesn't practise many values. We have a rise in discrimination, abuse of power, greed, etc. There is a big question mark on what we are leaving behind for our future generations. So, there is an utmost importance to know about values and inculcate them in our lives.
<|det|>text [140, 475, 861, 570]<|/det|>The "True Values" series, for classes 1 to 12, aims to impart good moral values such as kindness, courage, and compassion to build a child's character. Each book of the series will enable the learners to adopt values at the very core of their being and help them to become a foundation of their moral beliefs.
<|det|>text [140, 575, 861, 632]<|/det|>The topics are well organised with moral stories, ample exercises, and friendly exciting activities so as to develop creativity, problem-solving capabilities and communication skills in learners.
<|det|>text [140, 636, 860, 675]<|/det|>Sincere efforts have been made to make this series a fun learning experience, that the learners will carry throughout their lives.
<|det|>text [741, 678, 861, 696]<|/det|>—Publishers

<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 0, 278, 146]<|/det|>1
<|det|>title [372, 109, 948, 158]<|/det|>Controlling Emotions
<|det|>image [45, 211, 87, 240]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [105, 214, 277, 242]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [43, 259, 948, 325]<|/det|>Mona was a young girl. She was growing, she experienced chaotic changes in her emotions every now and then. She was not willing to go out anywhere or with anybody.
<|det|>text [44, 335, 949, 404]<|/det|>One day Mona shouted, "I am in no mood, mom. Please don't disturb me." Her mom came to her and held her close. "What happened my dear child? Why are you so frustrated? Aunt Rose wants to spend a weekend with you," said her mom.
<|det|>image [65, 418, 952, 803]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [55, 816, 960, 863]<|/det|>There was a frown on Mona's face, "Oh mom, it's so boring at Aunt Rose's house. I have no friends to play with. Moreover, I am in no mood to go anywhere today!"
<|det|>text [55, 870, 962, 915]<|/det|>She banged her feet and went into her room and banged the door shut. The poor mother sighed and worriedly sat on the chair.
<|det|>page_number [959, 933, 977, 948]<|/det|>5
<|det|>footer [896, 865, 999, 979]<|/det|>5
<PAGE><|det|>text [43, 59, 562, 81]<|/det|>• Find a healthy distraction and be positive
<|det|>text [96, 88, 945, 135]<|/det|>Find some creative things that interest you so that you may be distracted from your bad mood. Hobbies are a perfect way to change your mood.
<|det|>text [43, 144, 306, 166]<|/det|>• Catch your breath
<|det|>text [100, 173, 911, 195]<|/det|>Count to 10 or do something that lets you settle down for a few moments.
<|det|>image [414, 201, 583, 361]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [43, 369, 942, 416]<|/det|>Mona followed these simple steps. After a few days, she felt better and was able to cope up with her mood swings very soon.
<|det|>text [63, 432, 918, 456]<|/det|>Moral: Try to change your bad mood into good by following some simple means.
<|det|>title [60, 497, 202, 519]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [90, 529, 929, 614]<|/det|>Mood swing is a normal part of growth or adolescence and can be a healthy emotional response to outside stressors. Being able to choose the mood that is best suited to situation is one of the skills of emotional intelligence. Choosing the right mindset can help you control whatever situation you are facing.
<|det|>image [45, 645, 97, 675]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [105, 650, 238, 672]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>image [45, 688, 85, 721]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [90, 692, 447, 713]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>text [94, 722, 420, 743]<|/det|>1. What was troubling Mona?
<|det|>text [148, 748, 928, 781]<|/det|>Maha was troubled by the mood swings.
<|det|>text [93, 812, 559, 835]<|/det|>2. What did Mona's mother want her to do?
<|det|>text [148, 840, 970, 901]<|/det|>Muras Mother wanted she is visit our face's house and spend a weekend with wife.
<|det|>page_number [945, 928, 964, 943]<|/det|>7
<PAGE><|det|>text [105, 48, 660, 74]<|/det|>3. What steps did Mona's mother tell her to follow?
<|det|>text [162, 82, 960, 150]<|/det|>Mons's. Moth. lden. th. eat. well. sleep. went. and exercise. daily. to. gut. rid. of. bed. moved.
<|det|>image [65, 156, 96, 191]<|/det|>

<|det|>title [74, 159, 145, 177]<|/det|>Home
<|det|>list [74, 184, 418, 310]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [74, 184, 341, 207]<|/det|>My parents scold me

<|det|>image [31, 615, 66, 651]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [79, 619, 945, 660]<|/det|>Imagine you are competing in an athletic meet in the school. How would you cope up with your mood swing to do your best?
<|det|>image [50, 764, 150, 830]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [167, 787, 386, 808]<|/det|>Note for Teacher
<|det|>text [62, 822, 955, 886]<|/det|>Talk to the students about the ways and means to deal with bad mood. Ask them to narrate any incident when they had bad mood and how they made efforts to get out of that situation.
<|det|>footer [23, 937, 130, 988]<|/det|>8
<|det|>page_number [949, 940, 966, 955]<|/det|>9
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 28, 313, 142]<|/det|>2
<|det|>title [539, 92, 970, 143]<|/det|>Sportsmanship
<|det|>image [45, 206, 85, 237]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [108, 210, 281, 237]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>image [51, 270, 967, 626]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [48, 644, 414, 666]<|/det|>When the battle lines are drawn
<|det|>text [50, 670, 521, 694]<|/det|>And you have valiantly stood your ground
<|det|>text [51, 697, 329, 719]<|/det|>But once it finally dawns
<|det|>text [51, 724, 588, 749]<|/det|>That you have lost, then get off your high horse.
<|det|>text [48, 791, 490, 815]<|/det|>Step back with grace and accept defeat
<|det|>text [50, 818, 472, 842]<|/det|>It may be taken as a sign of weakness
<|det|>text [50, 845, 667, 871]<|/det|>But you know, it requires strength to gracefully retreat
<|det|>text [51, 872, 653, 897]<|/det|>And to congratulate your adversary and shake hands.
<|det|>page_number [34, 939, 62, 955]<|/det|>10
<PAGE><|det|>header [840, 24, 999, 90]<|/det|>[Non-Text]
<|det|>text [27, 72, 474, 146]<|/det|>You are enveloped with a sense of peace in life, love, sports or war, you win some And lose some, that's the way it is.
<|det|>text [27, 151, 692, 175]<|/det|>If everyone tries to have the last word, there will be anarchy.
<|det|>text [48, 202, 775, 230]<|/det|>Moral : Win or lose, showing good sportsmanship can go a long way.
<|det|>title [48, 269, 186, 291]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [72, 306, 916, 370]<|/det|>Sports bring out our competitive nature, but winning isn't everything. Play at your best. An act of good sportsmanship is remembered for a lifetime. A good sportsman respects both his teammates and his opponents equally.
<|det|>image [37, 402, 97, 430]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [105, 405, 228, 427]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>image [37, 450, 73, 481]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [84, 455, 433, 476]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>list [85, 489, 916, 808]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [85, 489, 581, 511]<|/det|>1. What do you understand by sportsmanship?
<|det|>text [138, 516, 307, 535]<|/det|>Sportsmanship
<|det|>text [84, 586, 641, 607]<|/det|>2. How should one behave if he/she loses in a game?
<|det|>text [84, 687, 563, 709]<|/det|>3. Why should one accept defeat with grace?
<|det|>text [84, 788, 520, 810]<|/det|>4. What have you learnt from the poem?
<|det|>page_number [924, 925, 950, 940]<|/det|>11
<PAGE><|det|>text [97, 59, 361, 80]<|/det|>8 Explain the stance
<|det|>text [191, 83, 620, 105]<|/det|>You are entiranced with a sense of your
<|det|>text [192, 111, 605, 134]<|/det|>It is the love, soars or war for me unto
<|det|>text [192, 139, 546, 161]<|/det|>And lose mine That's the way I is,
<|det|>text [192, 165, 815, 197]<|/det|>If everyone has to have the best books, please make my life to myself.
<|det|>image [95, 400, 138, 430]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [154, 404, 296, 427]<|/det|>Let's Act
<|det|>text [95, 447, 790, 476]<|/det|>A. To be a good sports player loves to be following qualifiers:
<|det|>text [139, 485, 300, 506]<|/det|>1. Play Fair
<|det|>text [214, 509, 714, 601]<|/det|>Never cheat or play dirty is me. Remember it's just a game. If you cheat, you may be victorious for a store room. But it will forever hurt your conscience.
<|det|>image [720, 481, 936, 606]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [134, 637, 406, 660]<|/det|>2. Be a team player
<|det|>text [212, 664, 698, 714]<|/det|>Do your best to get the whole team involved. Don't play for yourself, play for the team.
<|det|>image [720, 625, 948, 758]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [134, 763, 388, 786]<|/det|>3. Lose gracefully
<|det|>text [212, 793, 705, 864]<|/det|>In any sports, there will be winners and losers. When you lose don't sulk. On the other hand, give the other team congratulatory handshake.
<|det|>image [720, 789, 969, 916]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [55, 939, 82, 955]<|/det|>12
<PAGE><|det|>text [73, 83, 322, 103]<|/det|>4. Win with class
<|det|>text [168, 108, 596, 177]<|/det|>If you win, don't gloat or put down the other team after your win. Let your game performance speak for itself.
<|det|>image [654, 103, 885, 214]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [73, 238, 495, 260]<|/det|>5. Respect the rulings of officials
<|det|>text [174, 263, 655, 376]<|/det|>Remember to respect the rulings of referees and umpires. They may be wrong so make your appeal calmly. If by any chance they stand by their ruling, accept it and move on. They are also human beings and can make errors sometimes.
<|det|>image [676, 256, 895, 380]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [31, 426, 186, 444]<|/det|>Now read this.
<|det|>image [77, 454, 245, 600]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [254, 452, 926, 532]<|/det|>Raunak loves to attain the first position in everything he does. He loves to win and hates to be the runner-up. Whenever he loses, he becomes mean and argues unnecessarily. He is a sore loser and a proud winner. This is the reason he had lost all his friends.
<|det|>text [254, 542, 926, 604]<|/det|>Raunak comes to you for help. Tell him a story that will help him to be a good sports player. Include your thoughts on how he can develop the qualities of sportsmanship in your story!
<|det|>footer [882, 899, 991, 970]<|/det|>13
<|det|>page_number [924, 937, 950, 951]<|/det|>13
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 0, 999, 117]<|/det|>B Let's do a survey for you!
<|det|>image [131, 98, 241, 220]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [240, 120, 669, 141]<|/det|>What do you do when you win?
<|det|>text [158, 244, 517, 265]<|/det|>What do you do when you lose?
<|det|>image [887, 277, 976, 395]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [62, 393, 411, 418]<|/det|>C Am I a good sports player?
<|det|>text [100, 428, 993, 470]<|/det|>Answer these yes or no questions to see if you display good sportsmanship during games and activities.
<|det|>table [115, 485, 999, 786]<|/det|><table><tr><td></td><td>Yes</td><td>No</td></tr><tr><td>1. On winning, I congratulate others too.</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>2. I follow the rules of the game.</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>3. Everyone enjoys playing with me.</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>4. I cheer up my teammates even if I am not in the game.</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>5. Whenever I lose, I congratulate the others.</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>6. I listen to everyone&#x27;s ideas.</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>7. I have a positive attitude even if I lose.</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>8. I never trash ideas got from anyone.</td><td></td><td></td></tr></table>
<|det|>image [50, 804, 125, 875]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [180, 835, 404, 858]<|/det|>Note for Teacher
<|det|>text [70, 874, 999, 920]<|/det|>Teach the students the feelings of a sore loser and a sore winner and tell them how they can keep themselves away from being one of them.
<|det|>page_number [26, 956, 55, 973]<|/det|>14
<PAGE><|det|>image [0, 38, 268, 158]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [470, 114, 938, 162]<|/det|>Decision Making
<|det|>image [31, 221, 71, 250]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [91, 225, 260, 252]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [27, 269, 940, 382]<|/det|>A kid went into a candy store with his dad and was bedazzled by the treats that he could choose. "What should I choose? What should I choose?"—he asked himself. "Come on son, we don't have all day," his dad said. "These are my favourites," "no wait", "these are my favourites", he walked along the aisles, picking up bags and putting them back, he just couldn't make up his mind.
<|det|>image [8, 385, 990, 786]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [30, 791, 941, 914]<|/det|>"Quick son, make up your mind, we have to go",— his impatient dad said frantically. The boy ran around the store; his eyes moving from one shelf to another but all of the options looked so good that he couldn't make a decision. Eventually, the dad had grabbed his son by the hand and they walked out of the store emptyhanded. The young boy had tears in his eyes. He wanted them all but ended up with nothing
<|det|>page_number [931, 955, 961, 970]<|/det|>15
<PAGE><|det|>text [32, 58, 993, 282]<|/det|>because he couldn't choose just one. We are all that boy and, the world is that candy store. We have endless number of options available to us but sometimes we spend minutes after minute, hour after hour trying to find the absolutely perfect choice. We might spend days or even weeks trying to figure out which choice is best. Sometimes we worry about making the wrong choice. What! If we regret the direction that we take and it's too late to go back, the bigger danger is that we make no decision at all and end up going nowhere. This world is all about evolving, growing, succeeding failing and learning from those choices that weren't just right. Don't spend your entire life overthinking about the perfect choice because most of the time one doesn't exist.
<|det|>title [55, 304, 963, 352]<|/det|>Moral s Good decision making is the result of years of experience, learning from one's choices and that are both good and bad.
<|det|>title [53, 386, 204, 412]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [82, 424, 971, 511]<|/det|>Success in life depends on, decision you make at a time when it requires. After passing of time, decision making ability loses its importance just like a heavy shower after the drying of crops. So, don't spend entire life overthinking and waiting for a perfect choice.
<|det|>image [37, 559, 114, 587]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [108, 562, 238, 586]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>image [37, 603, 80, 631]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [84, 606, 451, 628]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>text [85, 637, 603, 660]<|/det|>1. What have you learnt from the above story?
<|det|>text [84, 733, 447, 755]<|/det|>2. How do you make decisions?
<|det|>text [82, 831, 692, 854]<|/det|>3. What do you do if people disagree about a decision?
<|det|>list [82, 637, 692, 854]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [0, 956, 35, 976]<|/det|>16
<PAGE><|det|>title [37, 69, 701, 98]<|/det|>B Write down the Positive and Negative aspects of decision.
<|det|>text [81, 107, 586, 128]<|/det|>1. Positive
<|det|>text [81, 143, 586, 164]<|/det|>2. Negative
<|det|>list [81, 107, 586, 164]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [37, 185, 310, 211]<|/det|>3 Experimental learning
<|det|>text [80, 220, 938, 260]<|/det|>Give an example of the decision you had made and what was the toughest part of that decision?
<|det|>image [46, 293, 150, 361]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [168, 324, 381, 345]<|/det|>Note for Teacher
<|det|>text [67, 360, 938, 463]<|/det|>Talk to the students about their feelings when they went to the nearby market to purchase an article, keeping in view what they liked the most but their parents denied saying next time you will get them OR because of lots of articles of the same kinds. Confusion overcame them, and they could not decide which one they should purchase and returned home emptyhanded.
<|det|>footer [896, 900, 999, 983]<|/det|>17
<|det|>page_number [937, 943, 965, 958]<|/det|>17
<PAGE><|det|>header [6, 0, 342, 146]<|/det|>[Non-Text]
<|det|>header [559, 95, 990, 143]<|/det|>Body Language
<|det|>image [72, 208, 118, 235]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [134, 212, 306, 238]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>image [78, 244, 986, 646]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [70, 662, 999, 778]<|/det|>Mrs Arora picked up the phone and said, "Hello! Good morning! Principal speaking!" The voice at the other end sounded a bit disturbed, "Good morning, Ma'am. I am the father of Ramit of class VIII. I want to talk about something serious. Lately, he has developed some mannerisms which I feel are not good. I know he respects you a lot and would definitely follow your advice. So, could you guide and correct him?"
<|det|>text [70, 783, 999, 828]<|/det|>Mrs Arora said, "Don't worry, Mr Khanna. I shall see that your problem is solved. Good day and take care."
<|det|>text [67, 836, 999, 907]<|/det|>She immediately started on the action after the call dropped. She went to Ramit's class and sat at the back, observing the class. Her eyes were at Ramit and his friends consistently. After a few minutes, she walked out of the class. She understood and
<|det|>page_number [55, 937, 82, 952]<|/det|>18
<PAGE><|det|>header [848, 6, 999, 75]<|/det|>[Non-Text]
<|det|>text [20, 66, 951, 140]<|/det|>recognised the problem. Ramit and his friends had started imitating a famous TV serial character and were not attentive in the class. Mrs Arora decided to speak to her students about the importance of correct body language.
<|det|>text [22, 146, 545, 171]<|/det|>Let's read what she explained to her students.
<|det|>image [42, 182, 936, 533]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [35, 540, 944, 652]<|/det|>Body language is the process of communicating non-verbally through body movements and gestures. This kind of language is considered the most important aspect of communication as it sends true signals about our feelings. As we perfect our own body language, we should at the same time, learn to interpret the body language of people around us.
<|det|>text [37, 655, 944, 704]<|/det|>Basically, our body language is controlled by our subconscious mind and is not always in accordance with what we are saying.
<|det|>text [38, 708, 944, 777]<|/det|>Hence, it is important to practise body language so that correct movements match our words. Thus, make sure that our thoughts and opinions are conveyed properly through our body as well as words.
<|det|>title [40, 784, 538, 808]<|/det|>Some basic tips for correct body language
<|det|>title [42, 818, 207, 837]<|/det|>- Posture
<|det|>text [104, 841, 808, 912]<|/det|>When we are sitting or standing, our back should be perfectly straight but not stiff. Let your shoulders relax. This will convey the idea that you are comfortable.
<|det|>image [832, 737, 937, 895]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [936, 945, 965, 961]<|/det|>19
<PAGE><|det|>image [0, 14, 136, 78]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [127, 60, 308, 83]<|/det|>Take up space
<|det|>text [124, 89, 829, 135]<|/det|>While sitting or standing, keep your legs a bit apart. This brings an idea that you are at ease.
<|det|>title [125, 142, 192, 161]<|/det|>Lean
<|det|>text [122, 171, 828, 215]<|/det|>Lean slightly when someone is talking to you. This will signify that you are an active listener.
<|det|>title [125, 224, 200, 243]<|/det|>Arms
<|det|>text [121, 254, 808, 345]<|/det|>Keep your arms comfortably to your side or bring your hands together in your lap to show others that you are open to the communication. Crossing your arms is a clue that you are turned off by what is going on around you.
<|det|>title [125, 353, 269, 372]<|/det|>Handshake
<|det|>text [121, 383, 823, 472]<|/det|>The handshake is an important non-verbal communication. It can set the mood for the entire conversation. A firm handshake is a sign of affirmity, credibility and positivity whereas a weak handshake will appear fragile to you.
<|det|>title [125, 481, 270, 501]<|/det|>Eye Contact
<|det|>text [120, 512, 744, 577]<|/det|>Proper eye contact during conversation shows your interest in the topic. Do stare at them but remember to look away occasionally.
<|det|>title [125, 587, 361, 607]<|/det|>Positive movement
<|det|>text [118, 618, 993, 661]<|/det|>Simple actions like nodding your head or smiling gives affirmation to your conversation.
<|det|>text [44, 673, 993, 720]<|/det|>Like we have learnt about these positive movements, there are certain body postures that give a negative impression. Read about them and try to keep them away.
<|det|>title [119, 739, 395, 762]<|/det|>- Looking at the ground
<|det|>text [115, 771, 808, 816]<|/det|>When someone is talking and the other person keeps looking at the ground, it tells that you are either shy or disinterested.
<|det|>title [115, 826, 341, 848]<|/det|>Checking the time
<|det|>text [113, 857, 806, 903]<|/det|>Checking the time signals that you don't want to be there, instead you have more important tasks to do.
<|det|>image [839, 75, 986, 250]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [851, 254, 977, 405]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [835, 411, 986, 500]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [761, 503, 986, 590]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [837, 600, 986, 710]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [834, 737, 986, 910]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [0, 924, 55, 976]<|/det|>20

<PAGE><|det|>title [102, 77, 204, 100]<|/det|>Tapping
<|det|>text [102, 107, 917, 132]<|/det|>Tapping your fingers, feet or any object like a pen shows your impatience.
<|det|>title [102, 138, 248, 160]<|/det|>On the edge
<|det|>text [102, 168, 943, 214]<|/det|>Sitting on the edge of the chair communicates your discomfort, both mentally as well as physically.
<|det|>title [102, 222, 234, 241]<|/det|>Fake smile
<|det|>text [102, 251, 943, 296]<|/det|>People get to know when you are faking your smile. So do not even bother trying. If you need to smile, see that it comes from within.
<|det|>title [62, 319, 919, 345]<|/det|>Tapping : Accurate body language is essential for conveying your thoughts properly.
<|det|>title [62, 383, 199, 406]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [90, 417, 927, 500]<|/det|>Body language is more powerful than words. It expresses thoughts and intentions through physical behaviour such as facial expressions, body postures, touch and use of space. Your ability to understand and interpret body language can help you to pick up on unspoken issues or negative feeling in others.
<|det|>image [53, 540, 108, 568]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [120, 543, 243, 565]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>image [53, 577, 98, 613]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [108, 581, 453, 601]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>text [111, 612, 485, 632]<|/det|>1. What was Mr Khanna's concern?
<|det|>text [110, 704, 672, 724]<|/det|>2. How did Mrs Arora check on Ramit and his friends?
<|det|>text [110, 798, 611, 820]<|/det|>3. What do you understand by body language?
<|det|>page_number [941, 933, 968, 948]<|/det|>21
<PAGE><|det|>text [113, 48, 643, 71]<|/det|>4. What is the need of correct body language?
<|det|>text [111, 155, 620, 180]<|/det|>5. Give some tips for correct body language.
<|det|>text [111, 336, 823, 361]<|/det|>6. What are some body postures that can leave negative effect?
<|det|>list [111, 48, 823, 361]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [113, 437, 293, 458]<|/det|>B Write Yes or No.
<|det|>text [113, 475, 856, 498]<|/det|>1. Proper eye contact is not necessary for any kind of conversation.
<|det|>text [113, 511, 635, 533]<|/det|>2. Leaning slightly towards the speaker is bad.
<|det|>text [113, 547, 650, 570]<|/det|>3. Crossing your arms while conversing is good.
<|det|>text [111, 583, 761, 605]<|/det|>4. Smiling is good for conversation, provided it is not fake.
<|det|>text [111, 618, 788, 641]<|/det|>5. Checking the time signals that you don't want to be there.
<|det|>text [111, 654, 690, 677]<|/det|>6. Sitting on the edge of the chair signifies comfort.
<|det|>list [111, 475, 856, 677]<|/det|>

<|det|>title [111, 706, 248, 730]<|/det|>Let's Act

<|det|>title [111, 755, 482, 778]<|/det|>Tick (√) the body language clues
<|det|>text [111, 789, 370, 845]<|/det|>1. Arms at your back and hands clasped

<|det|>text [631, 828, 780, 847]<|/det|>b. Nervous

<|det|>text [637, 70, 788, 91]<|/det|>a. Thinking

<|det|>title [65, 339, 661, 361]<|/det|>B Match the following pictures with its expression:
<|det|>image [191, 392, 280, 490]<|/det|>

<|det|>image [45, 717, 91, 755]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [108, 723, 350, 745]<|/det|>Experiential learning
<|det|>text [105, 751, 990, 794]<|/det|>Have you ever caught by someone through your body language, for example, in your class, when teacher caught you while talking.
<|det|>image [41, 803, 150, 880]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [172, 833, 398, 854]<|/det|>Note for Teacher
<|det|>text [74, 871, 984, 916]<|/det|>Ask the students to display different emotions like Sadness, happiness, thoughtfulness, anger, calm, surprise, etc. and let the other identify the feelings.
<|det|>page_number [14, 948, 41, 963]<|/det|>24
<PAGE><|det|>image [0, 48, 268, 168]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [462, 86, 936, 183]<|/det|>Be Cautious with
Computer
<|det|>image [45, 230, 81, 258]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [105, 232, 267, 256]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [42, 269, 936, 314]<|/det|>Sofia was busy doing her homework in her room when her father called her, "Sofia, come here."
<|det|>text [42, 320, 937, 386]<|/det|>He was in the living room. Sofia went to the room and saw her father's angry face. Rather it was a horrified, upset face. Sofia got scared as she knew something was wrong. She saw another man, nearly of her father's age, sitting on the sofa.
<|det|>image [57, 399, 930, 715]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [47, 728, 942, 790]<|/det|>"Sit down Sofia," said father. "This man here is to tell a disturbing thing about you." Sofia sat down, but she was terrified of what had happened. She had never seen the man before.
<|det|>text [49, 806, 492, 827]<|/det|>"Do you know me, Sofia?" asked the man.
<|det|>text [50, 835, 289, 856]<|/det|>"No, never," said Sofia.
<|det|>text [50, 864, 603, 886]<|/det|>"No? But I am your online friend, Joe," said the man.
<|det|>page_number [934, 923, 965, 938]<|/det|>25
<PAGE><|det|>text [89, 54, 993, 98]<|/det|>Sofia was shocked. "That's impossible. Joe is a girl of my age. And she lives in Toronto," said Sofia.
<|det|>text [88, 106, 993, 152]<|/det|>"Ha! Ha! Ha!" smiled the man. "I know. I told you all that but it wasn't true," said the man. Sofia was shocked.
<|det|>text [87, 160, 995, 295]<|/det|>"You see, Sofia, there are many fake accounts online and the account holders pretend to be someone else. They do it to cheat others and lead them astray. But I am a police officer. I came here to tell you how foolish you have been. You gave me so many details about yourself and so I am here. I had a friend whose daughter was of your age. She, fell into a trap of a fake account holder and got herself into a great danger.
<|det|>image [99, 304, 993, 658]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [0, 668, 573, 913]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [600, 662, 999, 798]<|/det|>Hearing so, Sofia was disturbed. The man continued, "You too, could have got yourself into a big trouble if you give out information online to strangers. They can turn out to be wicked."
<|det|>text [598, 809, 999, 925]<|/det|>Sofia burst into cries and ran to hug her father. The police officer said, "Never share your personal details online. You were lucky that it was me."
<|det|>page_number [41, 945, 70, 961]<|/det|>26
<PAGE><|det|>header [856, 25, 999, 99]<|/det|>[Non-Text]
<|det|>text [37, 72, 949, 140]<|/det|>“Sir, I will never do such a foolish thing again,” said Sofia. The man walked out of their house. She was thankful to the man. He had saved her from getting trapped into a dangerous situation.
<|det|>text [37, 148, 949, 218]<|/det|>We all should be very cautious while working with computers. Here are some set of rules or guidance for appropriate behaviour that we should follow while using the Internet. These set of rules are called Netiquette.
<|det|>title [77, 222, 226, 243]<|/det|>Netiquette:
<|det|>text [77, 251, 616, 339]<|/det|>1. Everything you post online is public. When you post things online, you are creating cyber fingerprint. It's irrelevant even if you delete it. It can be traced.
<|det|>text [77, 345, 615, 412]<|/det|>2. Mind your language and be kind. You may be looking at just a computer screen but there is another person on the receiving end.
<|det|>text [78, 416, 615, 503]<|/det|>3. Never post pictures of yourself or others unless you don't mind them being shared with everyone. Be very selective about the content you upload.
<|det|>text [78, 508, 942, 572]<|/det|>4. Don't give personal information online. Never share your full name, address, cell number, password, credit card number or any other confidential information online.
<|det|>text [79, 578, 941, 623]<|/det|>5. Do not use ALL CAPS when you are e-mailing or posting something. All CAPS is a form of internet shouting.
<|det|>text [79, 627, 942, 692]<|/det|>6. Don't post things when you are angry. Take some time to clear your head before you deal with the situation. This will help you not to say something which you might regret later.
<|det|>text [80, 697, 670, 719]<|/det|>7. Use different usernames or passwords for safety.
<|det|>text [80, 724, 942, 789]<|/det|>8. Set up an impressive e-mail address. You will use your e-mail address in future for college applications, job applications, resume and scholarship opportunities.
<|det|>text [80, 793, 867, 815]<|/det|>9. Never get into chatrooms and reveal confidential things about you.
<|det|>text [80, 820, 942, 842]<|/det|>10. Be honest and clear. Do not copy or claim credit for someone else's work.
<|det|>list [77, 251, 945, 842]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [65, 865, 497, 887]<|/det|>Moral : Be smart online. Be cautious.
<|det|>page_number [934, 935, 964, 951]<|/det|>27
<PAGE><|det|>title [62, 69, 210, 92]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [90, 95, 858, 216]<|/det|>The internet is one of the best resources available to us. Unfortunately, it's also extremely dangerous if you aren't aware of who and what lurks behind the scenes. Everyone should know how to be safe when surfing web. Ensure that no information is shared on internet which can harm you.
<|det|>image [868, 102, 999, 190]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [50, 260, 97, 291]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [124, 264, 251, 288]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>title [55, 309, 87, 333]<|/det|>A
<|det|>title [113, 310, 481, 331]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>text [117, 333, 944, 361]<|/det|>1. What was wrong when Sofia was called by her father to the living room?
<|det|>text [119, 427, 531, 449]<|/det|>2. What did the man explain to her?
<|det|>text [118, 509, 999, 551]<|/det|>3. Do you think Sofia could have got into serious trouble if her online friend was someone else?
<|det|>text [118, 616, 673, 638]<|/det|>4. What are netiquette? Why are they important?
<|det|>title [55, 762, 97, 805]<|/det|>B
<|det|>title [116, 767, 293, 787]<|/det|>Write Yes or No.
<|det|>text [117, 797, 556, 820]<|/det|>1. Everything you post online is public.
<|det|>text [116, 828, 797, 869]<|/det|>2. Never post your pictures unless you don't mind them being shared with all.
<|det|>text [116, 880, 588, 902]<|/det|>3. Share your personal information online.
<|det|>text [115, 911, 567, 933]<|/det|>4. Use all CAPS while you are e-mailing.
<|det|>page_number [38, 958, 65, 973]<|/det|>28
<PAGE><|det|>text [95, 89, 570, 110]<|/det|>5. An impressive e-mail is beneficial in future.
<|det|>text [95, 116, 763, 137]<|/det|>6. Do not copy or claim credit of someone else's work. Be honest.
<|det|>list [95, 89, 763, 137]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [41, 170, 77, 201]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [97, 171, 228, 193]<|/det|>Let's Act
<|det|>title [47, 226, 77, 246]<|/det|>A
<|det|>title [99, 225, 402, 244]<|/det|>Choose the correct answers.
<|det|>text [99, 245, 921, 286]<|/det|>1. You got an e-mail from a stranger with the subject "You are a winner!" What is one thing you should not do?
<|det|>text [152, 295, 292, 314]<|/det|>a. Delete it
<|det|>text [154, 326, 432, 347]<|/det|>b. Show it to your parents
<|det|>text [154, 358, 518, 377]<|/det|>c. Open it to see what you've won
<|det|>text [155, 388, 277, 408]<|/det|>d. Log off
<|det|>list [152, 295, 518, 408]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [99, 416, 925, 459]<|/det|>2. You received a WhatsApp message from someone you don't know. What should you do?
<|det|>text [154, 468, 645, 488]<|/det|>a. Do not respond and let a trusted adult know.
<|det|>text [155, 500, 405, 520]<|/det|>b. Ignore the message.
<|det|>text [155, 530, 709, 550]<|/det|>c. Text them to find out how he/she got your number.
<|det|>text [156, 560, 558, 580]<|/det|>d. Text them to find out who he/she is.
<|det|>list [154, 468, 709, 580]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [101, 590, 807, 612]<|/det|>3. Which of the following personal information is okay to put online?
<|det|>text [157, 621, 353, 641]<|/det|>a. Your full name
<|det|>text [158, 652, 395, 671]<|/det|>b. Your street address
<|det|>text [158, 683, 416, 702]<|/det|>c. Your favourite colour
<|det|>text [158, 714, 350, 732]<|/det|>d. None of these
<|det|>list [157, 621, 416, 732]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [102, 742, 637, 765]<|/det|>4. Before joining a group online, you should always
<|det|>text [157, 774, 375, 794]<|/det|>a. Ask your parents
<|det|>text [158, 804, 437, 825]<|/det|>b. Change your password
<|det|>text [158, 835, 369, 856]<|/det|>c. Tell your friends
<|det|>text [158, 869, 338, 889]<|/det|>d. Send money
<|det|>list [157, 774, 437, 889]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [931, 923, 959, 938]<|/det|>29

<|det|>text [97, 119, 775, 141]<|/det|>6. Do not copy or claim credit of someone else's work. Be honest.
<|det|>list [97, 92, 775, 141]<|/det|>

<|det|>text [98, 248, 931, 286]<|/det|>1. You got an e-mail from a stranger with the subject "You are a winner!" What is one thing you should not do?

<|det|>text [154, 385, 280, 406]<|/det|>d. Log off
<|det|>image [614, 280, 745, 375]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [98, 415, 931, 455]<|/det|>2. You received a WhatsApp message from someone you don't know. What should you do?
<|det|>list [153, 465, 718, 577]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [153, 465, 655, 485]<|/det|>a. Do not respond and let a trusted adult know.
<|det|>text [154, 496， 413, 516]<|/det|>b. Ignore the message.
<|det|>text [154, 526, 718, 546]<|/det|>c. Text them to find out how he/she got your number.
<|det|>text [154, 557, 567, 576]<|/det|>d. Text them to find out who he/she is.
<|det|>text [98, 586, 816, 608]<|/det|>3. Which of the following personal information is okay to put online?
<|det|>list [153, 616, 415, 732]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [154, 616, 354, 636]<|/det|>a. Your full name
<|det|>text [155, 647, 399, 666]<|/det|>b. Your street address
<|det|>text [155, 678, 420, 697]<|/det|>c. Your favourite colour
<|det|>text [155, 709, 351, 728]<|/det|>d. None of these
<|det|>text [98, 737, 645, 760]<|/det|>4. Before joining a group online, you should always
<|det|>list [153, 768, 444, 884]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [154, 769, 376, 790]<|/det|>a. Ask your parents
<|det|>text [156, 799, 442, 820]<|/det|>b. Change your password
<|det|>text [156, 830, 370, 851]<|/det|>c. Tell your friends
<|det|>text [156, 863, 340, 884]<|/det|>d. Send money

<|det|>title [127, 61, 344, 79]<|/det|>STAY SAFE ONLINE!
<|det|>text [183, 87, 603, 111]<|/det|>A. Put a smiley 😊 face for a friend
<|det|>text [183, 129, 595, 154]<|/det|>B. Put an angry 😊 face for a bully
<|det|>text [125, 172, 668, 195]<|/det|>1. Tags me in a picture of boys going to the park.
<|det|>text [125, 214, 367, 234]<|/det|>2. Asks where I live.
<|det|>text [126, 256, 446, 277]<|/det|>3. Uses bad words in a text.
<|det|>text [126, 299, 356, 321]<|/det|>4. Sends me jokes.
<|det|>text [126, 342, 515, 364]<|/det|>5. Sends an embarrassing picture.
<|det|>text [126, 385, 430, 404]<|/det|>6. Asks to meet me alone.
<|det|>text [126, 425, 625, 447]<|/det|>7. E-mails mean things about me to a friend.
<|det|>text [126, 468, 492, 489]<|/det|>8. Asks me about my favourites.

<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 38, 268, 150]<|/det|>8
<|det|>title [386, 114, 936, 153]<|/det|>Travels and Tourism
<|det|>text [87, 176, 879, 202]<|/det|>HIKING WITHOUT A GUIDE TO DUZKOU VALLEY OF NAGALAND, INDIA
<|det|>image [37, 220, 77, 245]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [91, 219, 266, 246]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>image [42, 270, 936, 458]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [40, 464, 942, 509]<|/det|>At 8,000 feet above sea level, Dzukou Valley is located in the northeastern part of Nagaland in India.
<|det|>text [41, 517, 941, 562]<|/det|>Having dreamed of visiting the valley since 2012, it was finally fulfilled in 2019, when I along with three other friends from Kerala and Uttar Pradesh went backpacking.
<|det|>text [41, 570, 941, 616]<|/det|>We refrained from watching any videos or travel guides to make sure we embraced the journey in its purest form.
<|det|>text [41, 622, 942, 667]<|/det|>When reaching Kohima—the capital of Nagaland—we ended up choosing the shorter and steeper Jakhama Trail for our Dzukou Valley Trek.
<|det|>text [42, 673, 945, 761]<|/det|>Beside a gushing river and the view of the highest of Jakhama Hills engulfed by the clouds, we marched ahead. Following the marked directions on the rocks, we ventured into a dense tropical evergreen forest. Splashing our face with the cold waters of a stream, we met a local who was descending the hill.
<|det|>text [43, 769, 949, 861]<|/det|>The local confirmed that we were on the right trail and motivated us with a picturesque description of the valley. Tuning into the sereness of the forest, we would lay on the boulders and sing. I bet on the 500 bird species found in Nagaland, a few of them did sing back to us.
<|det|>text [46, 865, 949, 913]<|/det|>The higher we hiked the steeper the train became and once it turned 5pm, the hills turned darker.
<|det|>page_number [941, 933, 970, 948]<|/det|>39
<PAGE><|det|>text [55, 42, 993, 138]<|/det|>Stepping on the narrow uneven precipitous trail with little left strength, we reached the top of Jakhama Hill to witness majestic reddish skies and the slumbering valley. We concluded the day dancing around the fire under the starry moonlit sky at the camp site.
<|det|>text [55, 146, 994, 263]<|/det|>The best thing about travelling without a guide or travel company was the fact that we could spend our time according to our will. We explored the valley for two consecutive days- walking into the unknown, dipping into the shivery waters of the Dzukou River, and sometimes only watching the valley for many hours from the top of a cave.
<|det|>text [56, 272, 993, 319]<|/det|>To have experienced the beauty of Dzukou Valley both under murky and sunny skies, and the romanticism of clouds with the hills of the valley at night was magical.
<|det|>text [56, 327, 994, 396]<|/det|>Tip: For flowers and views of the lush green valley against the clear blue skies, May to September is the best time to visit the Dzukou Valley. It snows from December to February, too.
<|det|>text [594, 406, 994, 430]<|/det|>—Joydeep from The Gypsy Chiring
<|det|>title [77, 454, 983, 502]<|/det|>Moral : Travel teaches you confidence, independence and freedom. It boosts your self-awareness and your problem solving skills.
<|det|>title [73, 537, 216, 560]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [102, 573, 988, 702]<|/det|>Travelling is the finest method to learn about ourselves as well as the world we live in. It brings cross-cultural understanding by impacting on cultures and communities. Life becomes an adventure through constant introduction to different experiences and meets individuals from all across the globe. Tourism has a potential to change the world by providing economic advantage to undeveloped areas and maintains the viability of tourist attractions.
<|det|>image [58, 742, 108, 775]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [127, 747, 256, 770]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>image [58, 789, 108, 822]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [120, 795, 483, 818]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>text [125, 825, 520, 848]<|/det|>1. What is the capital of Nagaland?
<|det|>page_number [37, 947, 65, 963]<|/det|>40
<PAGE><|det|>text [85, 51, 512, 84]<|/det|>2. Where is the Dzukou Valley located?
<|det|>text [85, 146, 895, 180]<|/det|>3. What is the best thing about travelling without a guide or travel company?
<|det|>text [87, 238, 520, 269]<|/det|>4. What is the importance of travelling?
<|det|>text [89, 331, 515, 358]<|/det|>5. How can travelling change your life?
<|det|>text [90, 422, 543, 450]<|/det|>6. How does travel affect your happiness?
<|det|>list [85, 51, 895, 450]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [39, 511, 87, 551]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [96, 516, 185, 535]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>text [105, 545, 930, 586]<|/det|>1. Write down the geographical and climatic difference of the places you have visited till now.
<|det|>text [104, 655, 639, 677]<|/det|>2. Tell us about the history and culture of the place.
<|det|>text [105, 744, 601, 765]<|/det|>3. Write about the local food and local markets.
<|det|>text [104, 833, 537, 855]<|/det|>4. What are the 5 main types of tourism?
<|det|>list [104, 545, 930, 855]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [924, 941, 956, 958]<|/det|>41
<PAGE><|det|>image [0, 0, 999, 999]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [62, 142, 235, 183]<|/det|>C I
<|det|>title [124, 163, 233, 183]<|/det|>Map Tour
<|det|>text [120, 177, 993, 222]<|/det|>Take a geographical map and drop points anywhere in the world. Add descriptions and photos from those points and publish the map tour in the class.
<|det|>title [62, 230, 95, 258]<|/det|>D
<|det|>title [121, 232, 441, 254]<|/det|>Hands on 'Make a Magazine'
<|det|>text [118, 263, 993, 326]<|/det|>This is a great group project. Students come together to determine the theme of the magazine, what to include, students' roles and tasks. The content should reflect the travel experience as a whole.
<|det|>image [50, 345, 108, 376]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [119, 350, 256, 373]<|/det|>Let's Act
<|det|>list [113, 399, 993, 486]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [114, 399, 558, 420]<|/det|>1. Collect 5 historical heritage of India.
<|det|>text [113, 424, 991, 461]<|/det|>2. Write about 5 travelling spots in India and the cultures developed surrounding the area.
<|det|>text [113, 467, 641, 486]<|/det|>3. Write about the economic aspect of tourism.
<|det|>image [50, 501, 156, 571]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [175, 531, 400, 553]<|/det|>Note for Teacher
<|det|>text [65, 569, 978, 610]<|/det|>Ask the students to collect tourist spots of India and discuss their economic and cultural importance.
<|det|>page_number [0, 930, 55, 976]<|/det|>42
<PAGE><|det|>title [255, 65, 671, 125]<|/det|>Model Test Paper-I
<|det|>text [377, 126, 558, 152]<|/det|>(Chapters 1 to 8)
<|det|>text [18, 178, 423, 204]<|/det|>A. Answer the following questions:
<|det|>list [65, 208, 570, 414]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [67, 209, 392, 232]<|/det|>1. What was troubling Mona?
<|det|>text [66, 268, 570, 295]<|/det|>2. What do you understand by sportsmanship?
<|det|>text [67, 331, 567, 356]<|/det|>3. What do you understand by body language?
<|det|>text [67, 392, 346, 414]<|/det|>4. What are netiquette?
<|det|>text [21, 453, 286, 475]<|/det|>B. Explain this stanza:
<|det|>text [336, 492, 560, 592]<|/det|>The rhyme of life
Is the grace and care
To brighten days
With kindness shared.
<|det|>text [25, 728, 267, 752]<|/det|>C. Write'Yes' or'No'.
<|det|>list [76, 761, 767, 890]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [76, 761, 767, 787]<|/det|>1. Proper eye contact is not necessary for any kind of conversation.
<|det|>text [76, 797, 516, 822]<|/det|>2. Always post things online when angry.
<|det|>text [76, 828, 682, 854]<|/det|>3. Smiling is good for conversation, provided it is not fake.
<|det|>text [76, 864, 463, 890]<|/det|>4. One good turn deserves another.
<|det|>image [848, 755, 919, 781]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [848, 789, 919, 815]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [848, 822, 919, 848]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [848, 856, 919, 881]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [924, 947, 956, 963]<|/det|>43
<PAGE><|det|>image [0, 32, 387, 155]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [361, 104, 990, 147]<|/det|>Save the Environment
<|det|>image [55, 214, 108, 243]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [122, 218, 296, 245]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [55, 261, 495, 285]<|/det|>Read this poem on saving the earth:
<|det|>image [71, 298, 337, 445]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [372, 314, 722, 336]<|/det|>Of all the planets, I am special,
<|det|>text [375, 342, 636, 361]<|/det|>I am the mother Earth,
<|det|>text [375, 369, 704, 389]<|/det|>Filled with water, air and life
<|det|>text [375, 395, 931, 415]<|/det|>Humans, trees, birds, water animals and wildlife.
<|det|>text [375, 422, 816, 444]<|/det|>I spin and turn all year round the orbit
<|det|>text [375, 449, 821, 470]<|/det|>To give you all the seasons and climate.
<|det|>text [55, 481, 543, 503]<|/det|>You people use my land, air, water and soil,
<|det|>text [55, 508, 676, 530]<|/det|>But keep on filling it with litter and allowing it to spoil,
<|det|>text [55, 535, 504, 556]<|/det|>I provide you air, food and home to live,
<|det|>text [55, 561, 580, 583]<|/det|>With greedy lust, you destroy the nature I give
<|det|>text [55, 588, 444, 608]<|/det|>And plot your own nature's death.
<|det|>image [55, 616, 353, 804]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [370, 645, 965, 668]<|/det|>The air you fill with smoke, makes my breath choke.
<|det|>text [371, 673, 753, 695]<|/det|>You make my heart fill with strife
<|det|>text [371, 700, 774, 722]<|/det|>But cutting down the green forests,
<|det|>text [371, 727, 807, 750]<|/det|>And pushing up the greenhouse gases.
<|det|>text [371, 754, 830, 776]<|/det|>What was once a clean sea and pure soil
<|det|>text [371, 781, 888, 802]<|/det|>Is now all barren, clodded with waste and oil.
<|det|>text [55, 810, 431, 833]<|/det|>How long can I stand the trouble,
<|det|>text [55, 837, 385, 857]<|/det|>I start to scream and tremble
<|det|>text [55, 864, 424, 886]<|/det|>I give you all I have, unto my end
<|det|>text [55, 891, 620, 913]<|/det|>But it's now all in your hands to defend and mend.
<|det|>page_number [38, 940, 65, 955]<|/det|>44
<PAGE><|det|>text [20, 65, 922, 199]<|/det|>The earth—our mother earth, is the only planet which has life. Anju opened her eyes and remembered that she had arrived with her grandpa at his estate the previous night. She heard the chirping of birds and was overjoyed. She ran out of her bed to see the birds. But oh! she felt disgusted. As she looked out of the window of her room, she could see a heap of garbage outside. She could smell the foul smell too. She felt really bad.
<|det|>image [24, 204, 903, 533]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [21, 539, 920, 608]<|/det|>She rushed to talk to her grandpa. Grandpa had just returned from his morning walk. The estate manager was by his side. Grandpa was instructing him to dispose off the garbage.
<|det|>text [21, 617, 922, 687]<|/det|>Anju wished her grandpa, "Good morning, grandpa." "Good morning, my girl. But the foul smell of this garbage turns into a bad one. I have been trying to find out ways for proper garbage disposal. But all in vain."
<|det|>text [23, 694, 922, 718]<|/det|>"Grandpa, we just need a cleaner surrounding to live peacefully and happily. Even
<|det|>image [24, 726, 481, 910]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [505, 719, 922, 784]<|/det|>our school contributes to make our campus cleaner as part of 'Swachh Bharat Abhiyan'.
<|det|>text [505, 794, 922, 860]<|/det|>"Yes, my child!" smiled grandpa. "This is India's biggest ever cleanliness drive to make India clean!
<|det|>text [506, 870, 924, 914]<|/det|>The only solution to this problem is 3R's - reduce, reuse, recycle.
<|det|>page_number [919, 937, 948, 951]<|/det|>45
<PAGE><|det|>image [0, 0, 103, 53]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [53, 45, 132, 64]<|/det|>Reuse
<|det|>text [51, 76, 521, 100]<|/det|>It refers to using items more than once.
<|det|>list [93, 109, 536, 196]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [93, 109, 536, 134]<|/det|>- Reuse plastic bags, cloth bags, etc.
<|det|>text [93, 142, 378, 163]<|/det|>- Reuse water bottles.
<|det|>text [93, 174, 517, 198]<|/det|>• Reuse papers for making covers.
<|det|>title [51, 204, 152, 224]<|/det|>Reduce
<|det|>text [51, 236, 519, 261]<|/det|>We should reduce the waste generated.
<|det|>list [93, 269, 999, 378]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [93, 269, 837, 295]<|/det|>- Replace plastic shopping bags with reusable shopping bags.
<|det|>text [93, 301, 934, 326]<|/det|>- Refuse to purchase products that use excessive packaging materials.
<|det|>text [93, 333, 999, 377]<|/det|>- Reduce the waste generated by using steel utensils instead of disposable ones.
<|det|>title [51, 387, 152, 409]<|/det|>Recycle
<|det|>text [51, 418, 415, 442]<|/det|>We should use recycled goods.
<|det|>list [93, 451, 999, 552]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [93, 451, 999, 497]<|/det|>- Buy products made from recycled materials or with recyclable or reusable packaging.
<|det|>text [93, 505, 999, 552]<|/det|>- Avoid packaging made of more than one material that cannot be separated or put in a waste recycling bin.
<|det|>text [49, 559, 999, 604]<|/det|>If we follow the 3R's appropriately, we can surely save our earth and make it a better place to live.
<|det|>text [71, 642, 770, 670]<|/det|>Moral : There is no other planet to live, so save the earth.
<|det|>title [68, 721, 216, 744]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [91, 759, 983, 854]<|/det|>What we take from the earth, we must give it back. This is the nature's law. Thinking differently, we all can change our habits. These small, worthwhile changes can create a great difference to save our earth. By being a little conscious, we can make a real difference for a better tomorrow.
<|det|>page_number [31, 957, 58, 973]<|/det|>46
<PAGE><|det|>header [19, 55, 67, 90]<|/det|>[NO TEXT]
<|det|>header [72, 54, 205, 80]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>header [17, 98, 55, 140]<|/det|>A
<|det|>title [70, 98, 427, 123]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>text [72, 125, 588, 158]<|/det|>1. What message have you got from the poem?
<|det|>text [71, 222, 331, 246]<|/det|>2. Explain these lines:
<|det|>text [164, 268, 687, 300]<|/det|>The air you fill with smoke, makes my breath choke,
<|det|>text [168, 302, 503, 327]<|/det|>You make my heart fill with strife
<|det|>text [168, 333, 524, 359]<|/det|>But cutting down the green forests
<|det|>text [168, 365, 565, 392]<|/det|>And pushing up the greenhouse gases.
<|det|>image [747, 280, 911, 390]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [80, 622, 435, 645]<|/det|>3. Why did Anju feel disgusted?
<|det|>text [80, 723, 563, 745]<|/det|>4. How can we keep our environment clean?
<|det|>text [82, 823, 377, 845]<|/det|>5. What are the three R's?
<|det|>image [840, 894, 999, 989]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [921, 945, 950, 961]<|/det|>47
<PAGE><|det|>aside_text [58, 63, 108, 101]<|/det|>8
<|det|>title [122, 68, 350, 90]<|/det|>Explain these terms:
<|det|>text [123, 96, 260, 116]<|/det|>1. Reuse
<|det|>text [123, 157, 275, 177]<|/det|>2. Reduce
<|det|>text [124, 219, 277, 242]<|/det|>3. Recycle
<|det|>image [58, 301, 108, 333]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [123, 307, 260, 331]<|/det|>Let's Act
<|det|>text [58, 349, 978, 456]<|/det|>We have heard the phrase "reduas, reuses, recycle". Yet too often we skip the 'reuse' portion. Wasting things wastes waste. Your challenge is to find the items that are quickly thrown away or placed into the recycling bin in your home. Think of a new use for each item. How can the item be utilized again before it leaves your home? How can using the item in this new way save you money?
<|det|>table [58, 468, 977, 913]<|/det|><table><tr><td></td><td>Item</td><td>New use</td><td>Savings</td></tr><tr><td>1.</td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>2.</td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>3.</td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>4.</td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>5.</td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></table>
<|det|>page_number [41, 935, 68, 950]<|/det|>48
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 24, 67, 99]<|/det|>8
<|det|>header [70, 45, 367, 70]<|/det|>Look at this poster on 3 R's.
<|det|>image [252, 78, 661, 380]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [654, 0, 999, 50]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [70, 396, 932, 461]<|/det|>Now you have been asked to participate in a campaign on "Effective garbage disposal for saving the earth" along with the children of your class. Make a poster here on the theme – reuse, reduce, recycle.
<|det|>image [26, 707, 64, 744]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [76, 714, 310, 736]<|/det|>Experiential learning
<|det|>text [74, 739, 934, 782]<|/det|>Do some basic things like – clean your desk. It will encourage you to support for cleanliness.
<|det|>image [34, 796, 107, 857]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [139, 830, 360, 851]<|/det|>Note for Teacher
<|det|>text [44, 863, 931, 907]<|/det|>Ask the students to make efforts to make our earth a cleaner and healthier place to live. Let them share their ideas and thoughts how they would serve for the same.
<|det|>page_number [931, 953, 961, 966]<|/det|>49
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 0, 307, 123]<|/det|>100
<|det|>title [420, 56, 986, 150]<|/det|>Failure – A Stepping
Stone
<|det|>image [62, 200, 108, 228]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [124, 205, 300, 232]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [60, 246, 601, 289]<|/det|>Failure is a common word. Many a times, we have to face failures in our lives.
<|det|>text [59, 300, 597, 390]<|/det|>But what does this word actually mean? Failure is actually the negative state of mind in which one sees no good and no progress. It is exactly the opposite of positive thinking and motivation.
<|det|>text [55, 399, 595, 533]<|/det|>When we think about failures, two contradictory thoughts come in our mind. One is that we forget about our achievements and keep on pondering over our failures. The other one is that we forget our failures and keep on pondering over our achievements. The first thought is a common
<|det|>image [57, 536, 337, 890]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [365, 536, 593, 622]<|/det|>one because it is the human tendency to think about failures. It can lead
<|det|>image [616, 253, 975, 616]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [360, 625, 978, 763]<|/det|>to depression even. It is a negative approach to life. But the second thought asks us to think positively in spite of our failures. It helps us to receive energy and think about the future enthusiastically. It's called a positive outlook towards life. It guides us to learn from our failures, to do better and to succeed the next time.
<|det|>text [356, 774, 976, 921]<|/det|>Generally, people are depressed by their failures. They are upset and this makes their lives painful and burdensome. They are not able to think positively and forget all about their achievements. But we should develop a positive outlook about failures. If you think positively, it will surely help you to reach success one day.
<|det|>image [0, 0, 999, 999]<|/det|>
<PAGE><|det|>text [20, 65, 922, 140]<|/det|>One should analyze his/her failure and its reasons constructively and work on them. This will help him/her to repeat such acts and once you know the cause of your failure, you can easily rectify it.
<|det|>image [99, 150, 812, 495]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [21, 513, 922, 629]<|/det|>In order to change our life, our failures need to be transformed into success. If we make a proper analysis, we will come to know that these failures can actually be very helpful. These are actually the stepping stones in life. Failures encourages us to work hard, so that we can achieve our goals and become successful in life. One who learns from his failures he/she learns to begin positivity.
<|det|>text [21, 639, 922, 709]<|/det|>No man is perfect. But we all are heading towards perfection. So, the failures are only breakers in life, but not a full stop. For human beings, it is natural to make mistakes and learn from them.
<|det|>text [21, 718, 922, 812]<|/det|>All the discoverers and inventors did not invent something in one go. They had to try many times and only then, they were able to create something great. Great writers didn't become famous with their first book, instead they spent many years of their life to get perfection and fame.
<|det|>text [21, 821, 922, 868]<|/det|>Everything starts from a simple idea and finally becomes great after a long chain of efforts and hard work.
<|det|>text [27, 876, 816, 900]<|/det|>However, in life, many times, we meet failures. What we should do then-
<|det|>page_number [919, 926, 947, 941]<|/det|>51
<PAGE><|det|>image [0, 0, 999, 999]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [102, 55, 270, 78]<|/det|>1. Accept it
<|det|>text [147, 88, 793, 210]<|/det|>What's done is done? You can only accept it now. Don't think about it again and again. There is no point worrying about something that cannot be changed. Instead, you should use your energy to affect the future by acting consciously.
<|det|>title [102, 221, 327, 242]<|/det|>2. Learn from it
<|det|>text [150, 251, 793, 298]<|/det|>The best thing is to learn a lesson from what has happened. This will help you to work better the next time.
<|det|>title [102, 306, 283, 328]<|/det|>3. Analyze it
<|det|>text [150, 335, 793, 356]<|/det|>Talk about the situation with your family or friends
<|det|>text [150, 358, 999, 404]<|/det|>whom you trust. They will surely give you support and guidance. You should also analyze the reason of your failure yourself.
<|det|>image [808, 56, 986, 216]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [808, 221, 986, 352]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [102, 414, 348, 435]<|/det|>4. Be grateful to it
<|det|>text [150, 441, 835, 463]<|/det|>Take your failure as a gift. Later it will make you feel better.
<|det|>title [102, 471, 620, 493]<|/det|>5. Prepare yourself for the next attempt
<|det|>text [150, 499, 999, 544]<|/det|>Use the information from your analysis and try not to repeat any of the things that led to failure.
<|det|>title [102, 552, 435, 572]<|/det|>6. Don't lose enthusiasm
<|det|>text [151, 580, 778, 601]<|/det|>You should try to become more enthusiastic this time.
<|det|>title [102, 609, 322, 629]<|/det|>7. Work harder
<|det|>text [150, 637, 760, 660]<|/det|>You should always work harder to achieve your goal.
<|det|>title [102, 666, 491, 688]<|/det|>8. Set your mind on your goal
<|det|>text [150, 695, 903, 717]<|/det|>Think positive, think success, and always be ready for hard work.
<|det|>title [102, 724, 367, 744]<|/det|>9. Have faith in God
<|det|>text [151, 753, 548, 774]<|/det|>Do it again. Do it now. Do it better.
<|det|>text [150, 783, 999, 829]<|/det|>Don't doubt your ability to achieve your goal. Keep your head up. Just trust God and pray to Him.
<|det|>title [78, 872, 705, 897]<|/det|>Moral: Failures are the stepping stones for success
<|det|>page_number [47, 941, 76, 958]<|/det|>52
<PAGE><|det|>title [25, 66, 154, 94]<|/det|>overview
<|det|>text [43, 103, 912, 193]<|/det|>Failure makes us sad. It's just the opposite of positive thinking and motivation. Every failure should be assessed well so that it can be overcome. Remember, to err is human. So don't worry about the mistakes. Just be positive, work hard and believe in almighty.
<|det|>image [21, 230, 64, 260]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [72, 230, 201, 254]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>image [21, 283, 55, 313]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [58, 285, 411, 308]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>list [58, 316, 620, 404]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [58, 316, 620, 341]<|/det|>1. How do you feel when you fail in your endeavour?
<|det|>text [58, 408, 560, 430]<|/det|>2. How can one transform failure into success?
<|det|>text [58, 498, 470, 519]<|/det|>3. What should one do if he/she fails?
<|det|>image [21, 587, 55, 626]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [60, 592, 246, 612]<|/det|>Fill in the blanks.
<|det|>list [58, 619, 912, 761]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [60, 620, 911, 643]<|/det|>1. There is no point ____ about something that you cannot change.
<|det|>text [58, 649, 834, 671]<|/det|>2. The best thing is to learn a ____ from what has happened.
<|det|>text [58, 681, 521, 702]<|/det|>3. Take your failure as a ____.
<|det|>text [58, 708, 600, 730]<|/det|>4. Failures are the ____ stones in life.
<|det|>text [58, 739, 517, 761]<|/det|>5. Think ____, think success.
<|det|>image [27, 792, 64, 821]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [74, 801, 216, 825]<|/det|>Let's Act
<|det|>image [27, 843, 58, 871]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [60, 849, 938, 918]<|/det|>Let's work hard through this maze. Try to go through it to reach the treasure. You might reach dead ends (failure) but keep trying through the other routes and complete the path and find the treasure.
<|det|>page_number [928, 955, 956, 970]<|/det|>53
<PAGE><|det|>image [302, 79, 770, 356]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [65, 375, 816, 482]<|/det|>S Try to recollect stories of great men who overcame failures with positive thinking. Those people can be our role models. Try to gain knowledge from their success stories. Also take motivation from those people who are struggling hard to win over failures. Remember that the greatest failure is quitting and not trying.
<|det|>text [65, 488, 816, 552]<|/det|>Think of a person you admire. Research and find an example of how he/she overcame his/her failures before he become successful.
<|det|>text [140, 559, 388, 580]<|/det|>Draw his/her portrait.
<|det|>text [640, 560, 925, 581]<|/det|>Write the person's name.
<|det|>image [114, 594, 487, 902]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [503, 594, 980, 922]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [0, 930, 77, 999]<|/det|>54
<PAGE><|det|>image [46, 56, 125, 123]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [139, 97, 885, 140]<|/det|>Describe how the person OVERCAME his/her failures before he become successful.
<|det|>image [26, 323, 60, 361]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [65, 325, 296, 349]<|/det|>Experiential learning
<|det|>text [60, 359, 935, 403]<|/det|>Write down some failures you met in life and what you have learnt from them. Share your success story with the class.
<|det|>image [26, 430, 123, 500]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [140, 460, 363, 483]<|/det|>Note for Teacher
<|det|>text [42, 499, 929, 543]<|/det|>Ask the students to come in front of the class and explain any incident in which they had experienced failure. Let them tell the class what they did to overcome them.
<|det|>page_number [934, 950, 965, 966]<|/det|>55
<PAGE><|det|>image [0, 38, 320, 162]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [499, 76, 977, 175]<|/det|>Health, Wellness
and Appearance
<|det|>image [50, 220, 108, 251]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [111, 225, 289, 251]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [47, 264, 980, 378]<|/det|>Once upon a time, there lived a generous and kind-hearted king. But people weren't happy with their king because the king was very lazy and would not do anything other than eating and sleeping. He spent days and weeks and months in his bed either eating something or sleeping. The king became a potato couch and people started to worry about the king.
<|det|>image [0, 380, 983, 791]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [33, 803, 980, 919]<|/det|>One day, the king realized that he couldn't move even his body, and his foot. He had become very fat and his enemies made fun of him, calling him 'Fatty King', bulky king, etc. The king invited expert doctors from various parts of the country and offered them generous rewards to make him fit. Unfortunately, none could help the king gain his health and fitness. The king spent enormous amount of
<|det|>page_number [0, 925, 77, 980]<|/det|>56
<PAGE><|det|>header [836, 4, 999, 63]<|/det|>[Non-Text]
<|det|>text [13, 58, 932, 518]<|/det|>money but everything went in vain. One fine morning, a holy man visited the country. He heard about the ill-health of the king and informed the minister at the palace that he could easily cure the king. Hearing these promising words, the minister became very happy. He requested the king to meet the holy man to get rid of his problem. The holy man resided at a distant place. Since the king could not move his body he asked the minister to bring the holy man to the palace, but the holy man refused. He said that the king had to go to him, if he wanted to get cured. After strenuous efforts, the king met the holy man at the latter's residence. The holy man complimented the king saying that he was a good ruler and assured the fatty king he would soon regain his health. He asked the king to come for treatment the next day. The king that he would be treated only if he came on foot to the holy man's residence. The king was unable to walk even a few steps on the road, but aided by his followers, he reached the holy man's place. Unfortunately, the holy man was not available there and his devotee requested the king to come and meet him the next day for treatment. This was repeated for two weeks and the king never met the holy man and never had any treatment. Gradually, the king realized that he felt a lot lighter, lost a considerable amount of weight that made him feel more active than before. He realized the reason why the holy man asked him to reach his place by walking. Very soon, the king regained his health and people became happy in his kingdom.
<|det|>title [34, 553, 687, 578]<|/det|>Moral : Health is wealth. If you feel good, you look good.
<|det|>title [34, 632, 169, 656]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [55, 665, 916, 711]<|/det|>Wealth is not all through which one can purchase good health, wellness, good appearance, peace and serenity. Only good food and exercise can keep us fit and healthy.
<|det|>image [23, 745, 77, 777]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [83, 753, 206, 777]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>image [23, 789, 64, 821]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [68, 798, 421, 820]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>text [65, 822, 456, 848]<|/det|>1. How did the king become bulky?
<|det|>page_number [928, 947, 956, 963]<|/det|>57
<PAGE><|det|>text [111, 63, 497, 87]<|/det|>2. Why was the minister so happy?
<|det|>text [111, 160, 548, 185]<|/det|>3. How did the holy man treat the king?
<|det|>text [110, 265, 503, 289]<|/det|>4. What was the nature of the king?
<|det|>text [110, 370, 562, 394]<|/det|>5. Why do you need healthy appearance?
<|det|>text [109, 475, 763, 499]<|/det|>6. Write down the five benefits of physical and mental health.
<|det|>text [62, 678, 199, 704]<|/det|>B. Let's Act
<|det|>text [100, 713, 978, 758]<|/det|>Do meditation for at least ten minutes in the class and share your feelings with your friends and parents after the class is over.
<|det|>image [58, 780, 153, 850]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [180, 812, 398, 833]<|/det|>Note for Teacher
<|det|>text [73, 849, 969, 892]<|/det|>Ask the students to form groups of five, and each group to present some useful tips for health, wellness and physical appearance.
<|det|>page_number [31, 943, 60, 958]<|/det|>58

<PAGE><|det|>image [0, 0, 999, 163]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [26, 226, 999, 593]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [259, 605, 725, 628]<|/det|>Healthy food is very important in my day.
<|det|>text [262, 636, 721, 658]<|/det|>My body needs nutrition to run and play.
<|det|>text [199, 666, 785, 689]<|/det|>Proteins keep my hair shiny and my muscles strong.
<|det|>text [251, 696, 733, 719]<|/det|>Carbohydrates give me energy all day long.
<|det|>text [238, 726, 756, 749]<|/det|>Fruits and vegetables have the vitamins I need,
<|det|>text [196, 756, 790, 778]<|/det|>Fats are good for my skin and help my brain succeed.
<|det|>text [235, 786, 753, 809]<|/det|>Dairy helps to keep my teeth and bones tough,
<|det|>text [232, 816, 756, 838]<|/det|>Milk, cheese and yogurt have all the right stuff.
<|det|>text [201, 846, 785, 869]<|/det|>A healthy balanced diet has all the food groups in it,
<|det|>text [236, 876, 753, 898]<|/det|>Combined with exercise, I'll be healthy and fit.
<|det|>page_number [931, 941, 963, 957]<|/det|>59
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 0, 999, 117]<|/det|>Morale: A balanced diet and exercise is the wonderful tonic of our life.
<|det|>title [66, 146, 212, 169]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [93, 179, 972, 225]<|/det|>We should observe healthy eating routine to enhance our physique and to keep us away from diseases. Exercises keep us agile the whole day.
<|det|>image [53, 258, 108, 286]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [126, 260, 254, 284]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>title [102, 306, 461, 328]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>list [113, 336, 960, 530]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [117, 339, 429, 359]<|/det|>1. What is a balanced diet?
<|det|>text [114, 435, 630, 457]<|/det|>2. What are the different components of food?
<|det|>text [114, 533, 463, 554]<|/det|>3. What do carbohydrates do?
<|det|>text [113, 631, 399, 652]<|/det|>4. What do proteins do?
<|det|>text [113, 729, 572, 751]<|/det|>5. What role do fats play in human body?
<|det|>text [112, 827, 598, 849]<|/det|>6. Why does our body need dairy products?
<|det|>page_number [26, 950, 52, 965]<|/det|>60
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 30, 999, 144]<|/det|>Let's Act
<|det|>text [17, 92, 936, 150]<|/det|>Look at the food pyramid that tells us about the food that we should eat. Discuss in the class and complete the balanced diet-chart for yourself.
<|det|>image [202, 150, 725, 481]<|/det|>
<|det|>image_caption [361, 489, 573, 510]<|/det|>Balanced diet chart
<|det|>table [73, 516, 920, 684]<|/det|><table><tr><td></td><td>Mon</td><td>Tue</td><td>Wed</td><td>Thu</td><td>Fri</td><td>Sat</td><td>Sun</td></tr><tr><td>Breakfast</td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Lunch</td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Dinner</td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></table>
<|det|>image [17, 685, 50, 721]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [58, 690, 586, 713]<|/det|>What will you advise in the following situations?
<|det|>text [76, 723, 97, 740]<|/det|>1.
<|det|>image [93, 771, 220, 910]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [218, 775, 380, 836]<|/det|>I have very irregular bowel movements.
<|det|>page_number [928, 950, 955, 966]<|/det|>61

<|det|>title [383, 95, 930, 137]<|/det|>Conflict Resolution
<|det|>image [8, 211, 51, 245]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [58, 208, 230, 237]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [7, 253, 926, 304]<|/det|>Once there was a father who left 17 camels as an asset for his three sons. When the father passed away, his sons opened up the will.
<|det|>image [99, 308, 843, 691]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [11, 705, 926, 773]<|/det|>The will of the father stated that the eldest son should get half of 17 camels while the middle son should be given one-third and the youngest son should be given one-ninth of the 17 camels.
<|det|>text [11, 780, 926, 872]<|/det|>As it is not possible to divide 17 into half or 17 by 3 or 17 by 9, the three sons started quarrelling with one another. How could they divide their father's inheritance? So, the three sons decided to go to a wise man. The wise man listened patiently about the whole matter of dividing 17 camels.
<|det|>text [12, 878, 926, 924]<|/det|>Then he gave a thought for a few minutes. He explained the division to the three sons.
<|det|>footer [863, 899, 990, 989]<|/det|>63

<|det|>text [7, 252, 928, 301]<|/det|>Once there was a father who left 17 camels as an asset for his three sons. When the father passed away, his sons opened up the will.

<|det|>text [11, 714, 925, 784]<|/det|>The will of the father stated that the eldest son should get half of 17 camels while the middle son should be given one-third and the youngest son shouldbe given one-ninth of the 17 camels.
<|det|>text [11, 780, 926, 872]<|/det|>As it is not possible to divide 1/7 into half or 17 by 3 or 17 by 9, the three sons started quarrelling with one another. How could they divide their father's inheritance ? So, the three sons decided to go to a wise man. The wise man listened patiently about the whole matter of dividing 17 camels.
<|det|>text [12,  878, 926, 925]<|/det|>Then he gave a thought for a few minutes. He explained the division to the three sons.

<|det|>image [92, 310, 845, 697]<|/det|>
