<|det|>title [388, 90, 930; Conflict Resolution
<|det|>image [8, 206, 51, 241]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [58, 205, 230, 233]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [7, 252, 928, 301]<|/det|>Once there was a father who left 17 camels as an asset for his three sons. When the father passed away, his sons opened up the will.
<|det|>image [96, 310, 843, 697]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [8, 712, 926, 784]<|/det|>The will of the father stated that the eldest son should get half of 17 camels while the middle son should be given one-third and the youngster son should be given one-ninth of the 17 camels.
<|det|>text [8, 786, 926, 873]<|/det|>As it is not possible to divide 17 into half or 17 by 3 or 17 by 9, the three sons started quarrelling with one another. How could they divide their father's inheritance? So, the three sons decided to go to a wise man. The wise man listened patiently about the whole matter of dividing 17 camels.
<|det|>text [9, 877, 926, 933]<|/det|>Then he gave a thought for a few minutes. He explained the division to the three sons.
<|det|>footer [863, 904, 999, 990]<|/det|>63
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 0, 236, 130]<|/det|>13
<|det|>title [388， 90, 930, 135]<|/det|>Conflict Resolution
<|det|>image [8, 205, 50, 241]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [58, 204, 230, 232]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [7, 252, 929, 301]<|/det|>Once there was a father who left 17 camels as an asset for his three sons. When the father passed away, his sons opened up the will.
<|det|>image [96, 310, 843, 701]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [7, 713, 926, 784]<|/det|>The will of the father stated that the eldest son should get half of 17 camels while the middle son should be given one第三rd and the youngest son should be given one-ninth of the 17 camels.
<|det|>text [7, 785, 926, 879]<|/det|>As it is not possible to divide 17 into half or 17 by 3 or 17 by 9, the three sons started quarrelling with the other. How could they divide their father's inheritance? So, the three sons decided to go to a wise man. The wise man listened patiently about the whole matter of dividing.
<|det|>text [8, 884, 926, 936]<|/det|>Then he gave a thought for a few minutes. He explained the division to the three sons.
<|det|>footer [863, 904, 999, 990]. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
<|det|>page_number [914, 955, 940, 970]<|/det|>63
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 0, 236, 136]<|/det|>13
<|det|>image [58, 68, 648, 364]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [680, 53, 972, 141]<|/det|>He said, "Let me add one of my camel to your camels. So you have 18 camels in total now."
<|det|>text [680, 152, 972, 294]<|/det|>Now, he started reading the deceased father's will. He said, "The eldest son should get half share. So, half of 18 is 9." So, he gave 9 camels to the eldest son.
<|det|>text [680, 296, 972, 361]<|/det|>Then he said, "One third of 18 is equal to 6." So, he gave the middle son 6 camels.
<|det|>text [55, 374, 893, 398]<|/det|>Then he said, "One-ninth of 18 is 2. So, the youngest son will get 2 camels."
<|det|>text [54, 405, 972, 451]<|/det|>The total was 9 plus 6 plus 2 which is 17 and one camel was left which the wise man took away and resolved the conflict easily.
<|det|>text [71, 469, 963, 515]<|/det|>Moral: There is a solution of every problem if one know how to solve it, in a proper way.
<|det|>title [71, 547, 216, 568]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [100, 580, 960, 622]<|/det|>Whenever, there are problems, there are solutions. If we think that there is no solution, we won't be able to reach any!
<|det|>image [55, 653, 114, 681]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [129, 657, 254, 680]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>image [55, 696, 114, 733]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [120, 701, 459, 721]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>text [111, 731, 449, 751]<|/det|>1. What was the father's asset?
<|det|>text [110, 816, 534, 836]<|/det|>2. What was written in the father's will?
<|det|>page_number [41, 935, 68, 951]<|/det|>64
<PAGE><|det|>text [60, 38, 806, 75]<|/det|>3. How did the wise man solve the conflict among the three brothers?
<|det|>text [60, 130, 472, 160]<|/det|>4. What do you learn from the story?
<|det|>list [60, 38, 806, 160]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [25, 236, 260, 268]<|/det|>B Write 'Yes' or 'No':
<|det|>text [62, 274, 740, 306]<|/det|>1. The father's will was the source of conflict among the sons.
<|det|>text [62, 309, 748, 336]<|/det|>2. If we think that there is a solution of every problem, we can.
<|det|>text [62, 343, 668, 369]<|/det|>3. The wise man was actually foolish to give his camel.
<|det|>text [62, 377, 631, 404]<|/det|>4. We should learn to manage conflicts effectively.
<|det|>list [62, 274, 748, 404]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [25, 442, 252, 475]<|/det|>Let's Act
<|det|>title [25, 495, 427, 525]<|/det|>A. Learn conflict management tips.
<|det|>text [344, 547, 611, 610]<|/det|>Listen to what is said and respect the feelings of others.
<|det|>text [55, 699, 325, 786]<|/det|>Know when to let something go. Conflicts cannot survive without your participation.
<|det|>text [387, 744, 573, 786]<|/det|>Manage conflicts effectively
<|det|>text [645, 692, 916, 775]<|/det|>Make conflict resolution your priority. It's not necessary to win the situation.
<|det|>text [55, 836, 325, 900]<|/det|>Decide whether the issue is really worthy of your time or attention.
<|det|>text [640, 826, 925, 890]<|/det|>Focus on the present. Never hold grudges because of the past.
<|det|>page_number [936, 948, 963, 963]<|/det|>65
<PAGE><|det|>title [57, 73, 963, 119]<|/det|>B Make this conflict resolution wheel and follow when there is a conflict. This way you will not only learn it but also stay calm.
<|det|>image [296, 128, 734, 425]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [43, 469, 87, 510]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [93, 473, 965, 565]<|/det|>You must have heard about conflict resolution from your adults. When it comes to conflict resolution, where do you stand, what do you do to be a conflict solver? Take this quick-self test and find out. Respond 'yes' or 'no' for each statement. If you get 5 or more yes, you are a good conflict solver:
<|det|>list [94, 577, 907, 910]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [95, 577, 611, 603]<|/det|>I listen to what the other person has to say.
<|det|>text [95, 611, 444, 637]<|/det|>☐ I'm willing to compromise.
<|det|>text [95, 646, 503, 671]<|/det|>☐ I try to calm down before I react.
<|det|>text [95, 679, 476, 704]<|/det|>☐ I avoid unnecessary shouting.
<|det|>text [95, 713, 515, 738]<|/det|>I speak the truth, even if it is rude.
<|det|>text [95, 746, 471, 772]<|/det|>☐ I speak the truth, but politely.
<|det|>text [95, 781, 629, 807]<|/det|>☐ I try to put myself in the other person's place.
<|det|>text [95, 815, 895, 843]<|/det|>☐ I look for ways to solve the problem rather than just win the argument.
<|det|>text [95, 850, 567, 875]<|/det|>☐ I do my best to avoid physical fighting.
<|det|>text [95, 883, 912, 911]<|/det|>☐ I try to see how I'm responsible instead of just blaming the other person.
<|det|>page_number [0, 932, 55, 976]<|/det|>66
<PAGE><|det|>text [0, 48, 670, 102]<|/det|>9 Think of a time when you had a conflict with your friend, parents or sibling:
<|det|>list [50, 109, 327, 202]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [50, 109, 303, 133]<|/det|>—Describe the conflict,
<|det|>text [50, 143, 275, 166]<|/det|>—Did you resolve it?
<|det|>text [50, 177, 327, 201]<|/det|>—How did you resolve it?
<|det|>image [702, 8, 999, 206]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [23, 400, 125, 464]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [134, 427, 360, 450]<|/det|>Note for Teacher
<|det|>text [50, 465, 930, 507]<|/det|>Ask the students to write a conversation between two classmates to effectively resolve their conflict.
<|det|>page_number [936, 945, 965, 961]<|/det|>67
<|det|>footer [867, 904, 999, 990]<|/det|>67
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 0, 327, 142]<|/det|>14
<|det|>title [574, 90, 983, 135]<|/det|>Foul Language
<|det|>image [45, 198, 91, 226]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [111, 204, 294, 232]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [44, 245, 725, 361]<|/det|>Rick was a nice young boy who had a very bad habit of getting angry at every little thing. He would easily get angry with anyone and would even use foul language sometimes. His parents and teachers often lectured him to control his anger and improve his behaviour.
<|det|>text [45, 368, 721, 416]<|/det|>Rick had a soft corner for paintings. So on his 9th birthday his mom gifted him a canvas and a palette of colours.
<|det|>text [78, 424, 361, 445]<|/det|>Rick : What's this for?
<|det|>text [77, 456, 721, 523]<|/det|>Mom : It's to help you express your anger. Whatever you feel angry about, you can paint it on the canvas to get rid of that frustration. Try it.
<|det|>text [47, 532, 474, 555]<|/det|>Rick painted a building falling down.
<|det|>text [45, 564, 721, 631]<|/det|>It's what I want to do to my school. Rick said I hate everyone there. The kids keep bullying and criticising me. I feel so angry with them.
<|det|>text [46, 640, 361, 663]<|/det|>Oh! Rick! Okay. Do it again.
<|det|>text [45, 672, 945, 799]<|/det|>Mom : Paint whatever you feel inside and whatever you think what to do. 'Rick took another sheet and started painting. From that day, whatever made Rick angry had to be painted on a sheet. By the weekend Rick had painted several disturbing paintings. His mom went to his room and found some of the paintings on the wall. She collected some and took Rick to the garden, with his paintings.'
<|det|>text [73, 801, 699, 827]<|/det|>Mom : Tell me Rick, what are these paintings about?
<|det|>text [72, 832, 990, 933]<|/det|>Rick : It's about those kids that always act so smart, they flash their expensive clothes and phones and make me look like a loser. It's about the teachers who keep saying to me that I don't perform well enough. It's about dad who always keeps me reminding of how well he used to do when he was of my age.
<|det|>image [735, 275, 999, 611]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [34, 947, 60, 962]<|/det|>68
<PAGE><|det|>image [11, 28, 999, 429]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [24, 430, 920, 486]<|/det|>Mom : Hmm, Now think and tell me what is the one thing common in all of them. Rick : I am angry at all of them.
<|det|>text [24, 493, 920, 549]<|/det|>Mom : Exactly! You reacted to each of those persons who said something bad about you. Your anger was in their power, not in yours.
<|det|>text [24, 549, 920, 626]<|/det|>Rick : You're right. Infact, while painting this, I threw the colours on the paintings, imagining that I was throwing something at them. I never tried to understand them and got angry all along without any reason.
<|det|>text [24, 626, 920, 738]<|/det|>Mom : Son, each bad word that you said to them left a hole in their heart. Those kids did well for themselves not to put you down. Your teachers tell you to improve because they know your potential and your dad compares himself with you only to inspire you, to tell you that if he was able to do it, so can you.
<|det|>text [24, 740, 920, 795]<|/det|>Rick : You're right mom, but what should I do when I get angry. It's always like anger overpowers me and I am never able to think properly.
<|det|>text [24, 795, 920, 893]<|/det|>Mom : Controlling anger and foul language can be simple only if you are committed to control it. Whenever you get angry, take a deep breath and count up to 20 before you react. Think happy thoughts and divert yourself from that situation. Slowly you will be able to control your anger and language.
<|det|>text [24, 890, 490, 923]<|/det|>Rick : Thanks Mom! I will try to do this.
<|det|>footer [863, 925, 986, 993]<|/det|>69
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 0, 999, 117]<|/det|>Moran: Everybody should be aware of anger and using foul language. They are enemies.
<|det|>title [86, 159, 231, 183]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [110, 194, 975, 243]<|/det|>Anger sucks up our energy and empties our brain. Foul language makes only enemies. We have to fight with them.
<|det|>image [65, 277, 130, 308]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [140, 284, 267, 308]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>title [65, 328, 474, 358]<|/det|>Answer the following questions:
<|det|>list [117, 369, 975, 808]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [117, 369, 603, 392]<|/det|>1. What gift did Rick get on his 9th birthday?
<|det|>text [116, 474, 794, 498]<|/det|>2. What was the reason behind Rick's anger and foul language?
<|det|>text [116, 579, 483, 602]<|/det|>3. What is the moral of the story?
<|det|>text [110, 684, 747, 711]<|/det|>4. What did Rick's mom suggest him to confront his anger?
<|det|>text [109, 791, 434, 815]<|/det|>5. Who was Rick angry with?
<|det|>page_number [41, 937, 68, 953]<|/det|>70
<PAGE><|det|>header [0, 0, 999, 117]<|/det|>Let's Act
<|det|>text [33, 116, 549, 147]<|/det|>List 4 ways how people use foul language.
<|det|>text [33, 308, 886, 339]<|/det|>List some suitable ways to avoid the use of offensive language practices.
<|det|>text [37, 508, 918, 575]<|/det|>Consider creating a swear jar. Every time you swear or use foul language. You put money in it. Give that jar to charity or to your friend. Now that will really make you clean up your language.
<|det|>image [0, 602, 103, 666]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [111, 629, 335, 650]<|/det|>Note for Teacher
<|det|>text [20, 662, 914, 728]<|/det|>Talk to the student not to become rude towards anybody, parents or friends. Teach them to some new way to calm down when they get angry. Anger harms mind, body and soul. So ask them to invent some new way to overcome anger.
<|det|>page_number [909, 947, 935, 963]<|/det|>71
<PAGE><|det|>image [0, 32, 340, 160]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [828, 109, 986, 152]<|/det|>
<|det|>image [65, 214, 118, 243]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [139, 221, 314, 250]<|/det|>Let's Begin
<|det|>text [62, 263, 479, 484]<|/det|>Once upon a time, there was a king who offered a prize to the artist who would paint the best picture of perfect peace. Many artists from all around came and tried. The king admired all the paintings, but there were only two of them which were near to what he wanted to see. Now he had to choose the winner between them.
<|det|>text [61, 495, 477, 692]<|/det|>One painting portrayed a beautiful scenery. It had lofty mountains covered with green vegetation and having a lake. The sky in the scenery looked beautiful because of the fluffy white clouds. The tree made the painting more attractive. To add to the peace content, a bird was sitting on the branch of the tree.
<|det|>text [59, 701, 476, 768]<|/det|>The second painting was that of barren mountains with no vegetation at all. The sky was black
<|det|>text [58, 769, 911, 793]<|/det|>and lightning struck the clouds. There was a waterfall on the rocky hillside.
<|det|>text [55, 789, 987, 864]<|/det|>Just a tiny bird was sitting in the hole in the rocky hill. The bird's beak was open so as to portray that it was singing some song although the surroundings seemed to be unpleasant.
<|det|>text [55, 874, 987, 921]<|/det|>Now, the king examined the two paintings and finally gave the results. He awarded the prize to the second one. He explained, "Both these paintings depict peace
<|det|>image [499, 268, 986, 755]<|/det|>
<|det|>page_number [41, 943, 69, 958]<|/det|>72
<PAGE><|det|>text [15, 48, 936, 129]<|/det|>beautifully, but real peace is not the state of rest that one enjoys in silent atmosphere, but perfect peace means one is calm at heart while in the midst of all unavoidable circumstances like noise, troubles, work and disturbances.
<|det|>text [15, 133, 651, 165]<|/det|>Everyone was happy to learn the explanation of the king.
<|det|>title [30, 189, 732, 223]<|/det|>Moral: Inner peace determines the nature of perfect peace.
<|det|>title [30, 258, 169, 283]<|/det|>Overview
<|det|>text [52, 296, 926, 386]<|/det|>Peace is the result of training your mind to face life as a challenge. It is basically the spiritual calm despite the presence of stressors in the outside world. Focus your attention on those things you can control and be true to yourself, then only you will find inner peace.
<|det|>image [21, 415, 87, 445]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [91, 419, 218, 443]<|/det|>Let's Do
<|det|>title [21, 464, 424, 496]<|/det|>A Answer the following questions:
<|det|>list [71, 501, 930, 644]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [72, 501, 565, 524]<|/det|>1. What did the king want the artists to paint?
<|det|>text [71, 580, 517, 602]<|/det|>2. Which two paintings did the king like?
<|det|>text [72, 658, 399, 680]<|/det|>3. Describe the first painting.
<|det|>text [74, 736, 434, 758]<|/det|>4. Describe the second painting.
<|det|>text [82, 811, 524, 834]<|/det|>5. What was the king's decision and why?
<|det|>page_number [928, 951, 956, 968]<|/det|>73
<PAGE><|det|>title [92, 73, 323, 100]<|/det|>8 Write true or false.
<|det|>list [111, 105, 655, 202]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [111, 105, 655, 130]<|/det|>1. Perfect peace relies on one finding inner peace.
<|det|>text [111, 133, 590, 157]<|/det|>2. Peace is the presence of burdens around.
<|det|>text [111, 161, 506, 186]<|/det|>3. The king chose the first painting.
<|det|>text [111, 189, 495, 213]<|/det|>4. Being at peace is not necessary.
<|det|>text [111, 215, 763, 240]<|/det|>5. Focus your attention on those things that you can control.
<|det|>image [55, 255, 96, 285]<|/det|>
<|det|>title [126, 261, 266, 287]<|/det|>Let's Act
<|det|>list [55, 301, 958, 470]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [55, 301, 478, 333]<|/det|>A Write an acrostic poem on peace.
<|det|>list [111, 334, 958, 470]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [111, 334, 958, 365]<|/det|>(1) ____
<|det|>text [111, 366, 958, 395]<|/det|>(1)  \( \overline{a} \)   \( \overline{b} \)   \( \overline{c} \)
<|det|>text [111, 397, 958, 425]<|/det|>(1) ____
<|det|>text [111, 427, 958, 456]<|/det|>(1) ____
<|det|>text [55, 482, 525, 506]<|/det|>F And we have a good time to have a party dinner.
<|det|>list [111, 510, 958, 653]<|/det|>
<|det|>text [111, 510, 958, 535]<|/det|>1. What did the king want the artists to paint?
<|det|>text [111, 580, 958, 605]<|/det|>2. Which two paintings did the king like?
<|det|>text [111, 660, 958, 684]<|/det|>3. Describe the first painting.
<|det|>text [111, 737, 958, 761]<|/det|>4. Describe the second painting.
<|det|>text [111, 811, 958, 835]<|/det|>5. What was the king's decision and why?
<|det|>page_number [924, 952, 953, 968]<|/det|>73
<PAGE><|det|>aside_text [58, 66, 96, 101]<|/det|>8
<|det|>title [111, 74, 323, 100]<|/det|>Write true or false.
<|det|>list [111, 105, 655, 230]<|/det|>
